body_en:
If your life is in danger, call 110, 120, or 119 first.

This guide explains which number to call, what to do, and how to find your embassy via official government channels — we do not list specific embassy phone numbers here.

Key numbers (nationwide)

  • 110 — police / safety / police report
  • 120 — ambulance
  • 119 — fire & rescue
  • 122 — traffic accidents

Language barrier

Operators mostly speak Chinese. Ask hotel staff or a passer-by to call for you. State your location first.

Find your embassy

Search <your country> embassy China or your foreign ministry website for 驻华使领馆.

110 is China's national police number — free, 24h, nationwide.

When to call

  • Theft, robbery, assault, missing person
  • Need a police report (《报案证明》)

How to call

  1. Give your location first
  2. Ask a Chinese-speaker to call if needed
  3. If detained, say: 我要联系我国大使馆 / 领事馆

120 is China's ambulance number — free, 24h, nationwide.

When to call

  • Sudden serious illness or injury
  • Cannot reach hospital safely alone

At hospital

Go to 急诊; major hospitals may have 国际部 / 外宾门诊. Call your insurance 24h line.

119 is China's fire and rescue number.

When to call

  • Fire, smoke, explosion
  • Trapped persons; hazmat leaks

Evacuate first, then call. For injuries also call 120.

122 is China's traffic accident hotline. You may also call 110.

  1. Move to safety
  2. State location and injuries
  3. Keep police report for insurance
